verdict: The occupation is moderately at risk of displacement by AI.

reasoning_steps.title: Routine cognition is high
reasoning_steps.body: Tasks 1, 2, and 4 involve routine pattern-matching on text/data that current frontier LLMs can handle.

reasoning_steps.title: AI capability gap is significant
reasoning_steps.body: Tasks 3, 5, 6, 8, 9, and 10 require human judgment and physical dexterity, but AI can perform tasks 2, 4, and 7 with high accuracy.

reasoning_steps.title: Regulatory moat is relatively weak
reasoning_steps.body: Task 11 requires diagnosing malfunctioning systems, which can be done by AI-powered diagnostic tools.

evidence_ledger.meta: US Dept of Labor task list for SOC 47-2111.00 (Electrician)
evidence_ledger.contribution: Task descriptions and O*NET task list

adjacent_careers.name: Electrical Inspector
adjacent_careers.reasoning: Requires human judgment and physical dexterity, but can be partially automated.

adjacent_careers.name: Electrician's Assistant
adjacent_careers.reasoning: Involves routine tasks that can be automated, but requires some human oversight.

recent_developments.headline: New AI-powered diagnostic tool for electrical systems
recent_developments.impact: Raises risk of displacement by automating some tasks, but may also improve efficiency and accuracy.

recent_developments.headline: Hands-on trades remain among the least automated
recent_developments.impact: Physical, in-situ work captures very little AI use today; trades have the lowest exposure of any major occupation group.

quotes.quote: We do not have enough plumbers, electricians, or HVAC techs. AI cannot fix a leaking pipe. The shortage in the trades is only going to widen.
quotes.speaker: Mike Rowe
quotes.title: Host of Dirty Jobs; founder, mikeroweWORKS Foundation

quotes.quote: The tasks most exposed to large language models are not physical. Plumbers, electricians, and construction workers have the lowest exposure of any major occupation group.
quotes.speaker: Tyna Eloundou and Pamela Mishkin
quotes.title: Researchers, OpenAI
quotes.source: GPTs are GPTs: An Early Look at the Labor Market Impact Potential of LLMs

quotes.quote: The world is short on skilled tradespeople and we have spent two generations telling kids the only path is a four-year degree. AI is now coming for the desk jobs first.
quotes.speaker: Mark Cuban
quotes.title: Investor; co-founder, Cost Plus Drugs
